rankings.title: The CROWN Act and Black Worker Outcomes: Does Banning Hair Discrimination Reduce Racial Employment Gaps?
conditions: demonstrating first-stage/bite using complaints
job postings
or HR policy text
pre-registering a small number of primary outcomes
showing robustness to dropping 2020
to border-county comparisons

rankings.title: Breaking the Anchor: Salary History Bans and Worker-Firm Matching Efficiency
conditions: adding a direct first-stage measure—job postings with “salary history” language
recruiter surveys
or enforcement/complaint data
tightening the matching object to 1–2 high-credibility measures

rankings.title: State Domestic Violence Leave Laws and Female Labor Force Attachment
conditions: finding a dataset that better targets the treated group—e.g.
linked administrative records from DV service providers/courts
or credible proxies with validated first-stage—
harmonizing policy strength into a transparent treatment index

rankings.title: Cleared to Work: State Cannabis Employment Testing Bans and Labor Market Tightness
Idea 1: The CROWN Act and Black Worker Outcomes
conditions: proving parallel trends hold through the COVID shock
demonstrating a clear mechanism bite via occupational sorting rather than just aggregate employment

rankings.title: Idea 3: Breaking the Anchor: Salary History Bans and Worker-Firm Matching Efficiency
conditions: abandoning the CPS
securing a massive administrative dataset—like LEHD
ADP payroll data
or universe tax records—to actually power the job-switching mechanism

rankings.title: Idea 2: Cleared to Work: State Cannabis Employment Testing Bans
Idea 4: State Domestic Violence Leave Laws and Female Labor Force Attachment
Idea 1: The CROWN Act and Black Worker Outcomes: Does Banning Hair Discrimination Reduce Racial Employment Gaps?
conditions: confirming no pre-trends in triple-diff event studies
piloting mechanism decomp for at least one clear substitution like occupational upgrading

rankings.title: Idea 2: Cleared to Work: State Cannabis Employment Testing Bans and Labor Market Tightness
conditions: can stack with firm-level QCEW for scale
show MDE rules out small effects

conditions: linking to NCVS for victim subsample or bounds
prioritizing job retention mechanism
